data.title: Bank of America in charts: Average loans and leases increased 4% in Q1
Gilat climbs 5% secures multimillion defense order
Trump administration freezes over $2B in federal funding for Harvard University
data.description:
The Trump administration has frozen $2.2B in multi-year grants and $60M in multi-year contract value to Harvard University, after the Ivy League school refused to meet its demands for policy changes.

The White House had been reviewing $9B in federal funding for Harvard as part of its crackdown on alleged antisemitism on college campuses. Harvard was directed to comply with a list of demands to ensure federal funding, including shutting down diversity programs and restricting protests.

"Although some demands outlined by the government are aimed at combating antisemitism, the majority represent direct governmental regulation of the 'intellectual conditions' at Harvard," the university's president Alan Garber said in a statement, released hours before the federal funding freeze was announced.

"The university will not surrender its independence or relinquish its constitutional rights," Garber added. "No government - regardless of which party is in power - should dictate what private universities can teach, whom they can admit and hire, and which areas of study and inquiry they can pursue." 

"Harvard's statement reinforces the troubling entitlement mindset that is endemic in our nation's most prestigious universities – that federal investment does not come with the responsibility to uphold civil rights laws," the Department of Education's Joint Task Force to Combat Anti-Semitism responded.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Bank of America GAAP EPS of $0.90 beats by $0.08, revenue of $27.37B beats by $460M
data.description:
• Bank of America press release (NYSE:BAC): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.90 beats by $0.08.
  • Revenue of $27.37B (+6.0% Y/Y) beats by $460M.
  • Revenue, net of interest expense, of $27.4 billion ($27.5 billion FTE), up 6%, driven by noninterest income growth across all segments and higher net interest income
  • • Provision for credit losses of $1.5 billion increased from $1.3 billion in 1Q24 and was flat from 4Q24
  • Book value per common share rose 8% to $36.39; tangible book value per common share rose 9% to $27.12
  • Shares +1% PM.

data.title: Citi strategist sharply cuts S&P 500 target
data.description:
Citi equity strategist Scott Chronert cut his year-end target for U.S. large-cap stocks in the face of uncertainty caused by tariff policy.

Chronert cut his S&P 500 (SP500) (NYSEARCA:SPY) (NYSEARCA:IVV) (NYSEARCA:VOO) target to 5,800 for the end of 2025 from 6,500. He also cut his EPS forecast for the index as a whole to $255 from $270.

“No doubt, the goldilocks sentiment in place entering this year has given way to abject uncertainty,” Chronert said.

The trade system in place since China joined the WTO in 2001 will be disrupted by the White House tariff approach, he added.

The move comes as Citi's global equity team cut the U.S. market to Neutral from Outperform, saying the cases for "exceptionalism" are fading and tariffs could damage U.S. EPS the most.
